# ThermoPheno

**Thermal-time-based crop phenology model for historical analysis,
climate-change impact assessment, and real-data validation.**

ThermoPheno simulates crop planting, maturity, and harvest timing using
daily minimum and maximum temperature data. It supports simple, capped,
and triangular thermal-time formulations, summer and winter crop logic,
dynamic planting windows, forced harvest rules, and scenario comparison.

## Installation

``` r

if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("MR-Eini/ThermoPheno")
```

## Launch the Shiny app

``` r

library(ThermoPheno)
run_thermopheno_app()
```

The shorter alias also works:

``` r

ThermoPheno()
```

## Example data

Example files are bundled in `inst/extdata`:

``` r

system.file("extdata", "Germany_historical_1981_2010_dummy_data.csv", package = "ThermoPheno")
system.file("extdata", "Germany_10_scenarios_2071_2100_dummy_data.csv", package = "ThermoPheno")
```

## Minimal model example

``` r

library(ThermoPheno)

weather_file <- system.file(
  "extdata", "Germany_historical_1981_2010_dummy_data.csv",
  package = "ThermoPheno"
)
weather <- prepare_weather(read.csv(weather_file))

req <- estimate_required_tt(
  weather = weather,
  baseline_years = 1981:2010,
  planting_mmdd = "04-15",
  days_to_maturity = 140,
  t_base = 8,
  t_opt = 25,
  t_max_cut = 35,
  tt_mode = "triangular",
  crop_type = "summer"
)

sim <- run_simulation(
  weather = weather,
  crop_name = "Maize",
  required_tt = req$required_tt,
  earliest_planting_mmdd = "03-15",
  latest_planting_mmdd = "05-31",
  latest_harvest_mmdd = "10-01",
  t_base = 8,
  t_opt = 25,
  t_max_cut = 35,
  tt_mode = "triangular",
  crop_type = "summer",
  min_mean_temp_plant = 8
)

head(sim)
```

## Real-data validation

A DWD validation workflow is included in `validation/`. It downloads
crop phenology observations and daily climate records, pairs phenology
stations with nearest DWD climate stations, runs ThermoPheno, and
exports validation metrics and figures.

``` r

source("validation/dwd_validate_thermopheno.R")
```

Results are written to:

``` text
validation/results/
```

## Local checks

``` r

source("RUN_CHECKS.R")
```

## Main assumptions

- Crop development is driven by air temperature only.
- Daily mean temperature is calculated as `(tmin + tmax) / 2`.
- The current winter-crop logic includes simple vernalization and
  dormancy rules.
- Photoperiod, radiation, water stress, cultivar differences, and
  management behaviour are not explicitly modelled.

## License

MIT.

# Package index

## Launching the app

- [`ThermoPheno()`](https://mr-eini.github.io/ThermoPheno-dev/reference/ThermoPheno.md)
  : Launch the ThermoPheno Shiny application
- [`run_thermopheno_app()`](https://mr-eini.github.io/ThermoPheno-dev/reference/run_thermopheno_app.md)
  : Launch the ThermoPheno Shiny application

## Core model functions

- [`prepare_weather()`](https://mr-eini.github.io/ThermoPheno-dev/reference/prepare_weather.md)
  : Prepare daily weather data
- [`calc_daily_tt()`](https://mr-eini.github.io/ThermoPheno-dev/reference/calc_daily_tt.md)
  : Calculate daily thermal time
- [`estimate_required_tt()`](https://mr-eini.github.io/ThermoPheno-dev/reference/estimate_required_tt.md)
  : Estimate required thermal time
- [`find_planting_date()`](https://mr-eini.github.io/ThermoPheno-dev/reference/find_planting_date.md)
  : Find planting date
- [`simulate_one_year()`](https://mr-eini.github.io/ThermoPheno-dev/reference/simulate_one_year.md)
  : Simulate one crop season
- [`run_simulation()`](https://mr-eini.github.io/ThermoPheno-dev/reference/run_simulation.md)
  : Run crop simulations
- [`default_parameters()`](https://mr-eini.github.io/ThermoPheno-dev/reference/default_parameters.md)
  : Default crop parameters

## Validation helper

- [`compare_validation_metrics()`](https://mr-eini.github.io/ThermoPheno-dev/reference/compare_validation_metrics.md)
  : Compare observed and simulated dates
